more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: NAP pushes ‘lithium-for-fuel’ policy amid price surge
more.description: Source: NAP pushes ‘lithium-for-fuel’ policy amid price surge –Newsday Zimbabwe HARARE, Mar. 20 (NewsDay Live) – The Nationalists Alliance Party (NAP) has sounded the alarm over surging fuel prices, warning of severe knock-on effects for households and businesses already strained by rising costs. In a statement released Friday, party president Divine Mhambi Hove said the [...] The post NAP pushes ‘lithium-for-fuel’ policy amid price surge appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Zim fuel now second most expensive in Sadc
more.description: Source: Zim fuel now second most expensive in Sadc -Newsday Zimbabwe ZIMBABWE’S markets, businesses and motorists have been jolted by steep government fuel price hikes, with diesel and petrol surging nearly 16% and 27% per litre, respectively, to US$2,05 and US$2,17, intensifying cost and inflationary pressures across the economy. On Wednesday, the government, through the Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority [...] The post Zim fuel now second most expensive in Sadc appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Former minister loses land dispute
more.description: Source: Former minister loses land dispute –Newsday Zimbabwe FORMER Women Affairs, Gender and Community Development minister Nyasha Chikwinya has lost a legal battle against a housing co-operative over a prime piece of land in Goromonzi district, with the High Court ordering her eviction and declaring her offer letter invalid. The dispute was centred on a 150,27-hectare property, known as the remainder of Glen [...] The post Former minister loses land dispute appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Treasury releases funding to GMB to clear outstanding arrears
more.description: Source: Treasury releases funding to GMB to clear outstanding arrears -Newsday Zimbabwe THE Grain Marketing Board (GMB) has received ZiG60 million and US$5 million from Treasury to facilitate payment to farmers amid long-standing concerns over delays. GMB said the funds were part of ongoing efforts to ensure timely payment to farmers and maintain confidence in the country’s grain marketing system. “This [...] The post Treasury releases funding to GMB to clear outstanding arrears appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Women in STEM spark rise in female scientists
more.description: Source: Women in STEM spark rise in female scientists -Newsday Zimbabwe WOMEN in Science, Technology and Innovation (WiSTI) are driving a steady rise in women’s participation in STEM fields through targeted mentorship, leadership development and knowledge sharing. STEM is an acronym for Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics. more.title: BREAKING: Bread prices surge
more.description: Source: BREAKING: Bread prices surge -Newsday Zimbabwe HARARE, Mar. 20 (NewsDay Live) – The price of a standard loaf of bread has risen nationwide, with a snap survey showing it now selling for between US$1 and US$1.17, as higher fuel costs ripple through production and distribution. more.title: ‘Chinese firms take over Zim’s cement industry’
more.description: Source: ‘Chinese firms take over Zim’s cement industry’ -Newsday Zimbabwe CHINESE firms now largely dominate Zimbabwe’s cement industry, with six operating locally compared to just two domestic producers, highlighting Beijing’s expanding influence in the country’s strategic sectors beyond mining. China dominates Zimbabwe’s lithium industry via Sinomine Resource Group, Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt, and Suzhou TA&A Ultra [...] The post ‘Chinese firms take over Zim’s cement industry’ appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: ‘Red Cross first line of defence in disasters’
more.description: Source: ‘Red Cross first line of defence in disasters’ – herald Theseus Shambare THE Zimbabwe Red Cross Society has been described as the country’s “first line of defence” in times of disaster, as Government called for stronger partnerships and proactive approaches to address escalating humanitarian challenges. more.title: Govt explores ways to reduce fuel price burden on people

more.title: Political interference hampering enforcement of strict regulations on trucks says Transport Minister
more.description: POLITICAL connections are hindering government’s efforts to implement and enforce tighter regulations on overloaded trucks that use Zimbabwe’s main roads, Minister of Transport, Felix Mhona has said. Mhona was speaking in the National Assembly when legislators questioned him on measures being taken to address the issue of overloaded lorries plying the country’s routes. Mhona hinted [...] The post Political interference hampering enforcement of strict regulations on trucks says Transport Minister appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Veteran journalist Loughty Dube elected chairperson of regional press council
more.description: Veteran journalist Loughty Dube, who heads the Voluntary Media Council of Zimbabwe (VMCZ), has been elected inaugural chairperson of the newly formed Southern African Press Councils (SAPC), marking a significant step in regional efforts to strengthen media accountability and press freedom. Dube was elected at the body’s launch in Lusaka, where press councils and media [...] The post Veteran journalist Loughty Dube elected chairperson of regional press council appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .
